title: anthyllis leguminosa
intro: Anthyllis leguminosa, also known as kidney vetch, is a perennial legume native to Europe, Asia, and North Africa, characterized by its pink or purple flowers and red or purple pods containing seeds. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and diuretic properties, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and coumarins. The health benefits of Anthyllis leguminosa include its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of urinary tract issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. Anthyllis leguminosa is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and can also be found in herbal tinctures and capsules, which are used to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
